name: cycle
description: Autonomous work-loop driver scoped to the session's ACTIVE DOMAIN (commons @D g58). Four commands: /cycle — read the active <NAME>.md snapshot, enumerate its open `- [ ]` milestones (off-domain work is forbidden; if no active domain, prompt for `/domain set <NAME>` first); if the next-list is empty, auto-seed ≤3 candidate milestones from recent-context signals (prior-turn /gap priority shortlist · explicit user hint · log-tail open thread) via `/domain milestone <text>` per item, then re-enumerate (no-signal case stops with steer-options rather than fabricating); → dup-race precheck (3-signal grep: INBOX.log.md entry status + merged PR + git log) → parallel-plan table → fan-out (one background Agent per item, same message) → loop. /cycle-full — depletion brainstorm (rounds until no novel ideas, cap 8) on $ARGUMENTS within the active domain, then the same precheck → plan-table → fan-out → loop tail (phase 0 is one-time per goal; subsequent rounds use plain /cycle). /cycle-loop — thin wrapper that hands /cycle off to the `loop` skill (dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing) so rounds fire automatically until milestones deplete or user halts. /cycle-full-loop — /cycle-full once (one-time brainstorm + first fan-out) then hands off to /loop /cycle for subsequent rounds. Distinct from all-bg-go (reactive fan-out of PRIOR-turn branches; /cycle self-generates the next batch each round). NL triggers: /cycle · /cycle-full · /cycle-loop · /cycle-full-loop · 사이클 · 계속 진행 · 다음 라운드 · keep cycling · march on.
